title: Dashboard: Database Context Management (View/Edit DB Entities)
description: Enable viewing and editing of agent definitions, instruction files, and skill definitions stored in the MCP database via the dashboard. This includes updating the dashboard server API to support CRUD on these tables and enhancing the dashboard UI with database-specific views and editor integration.
goals: Dashboard server API supports full CRUD for agent_definitions, instruction_files, and skill_definitions in the DB.
Dashboard UI provides a dedicated view for DB-stored context files (Agents, Instructions, Skills).
User can edit and save DB-stored context content directly from the dashboard.
Changes to DB context are reflected immediately in the system (verified via re-seeding or direct DB check).
success_criteria: `/api/agents/db`, `/api/instructions/db`, and `/api/skills/db` (and their PUT/GET :id counterparts) return correct data and successfully update the DB.
The dashboard UI correctly displays all agents, instructions, and skills from the database when the DB view is active.
Saving a modification in the dashboard editor updates the corresponding row in the database (verified by checking `updated_at` and content).
All TypeScript errors in the modified dashboard files are resolved and the frontend builds successfully.

steps.task: Enhance `dashboard/server/src/db/queries.ts` with list/get/update/delete functions for `agent_definitions`, `instruction_files`, and `skill_definitions`.
steps.notes: Enhanced `dashboard/server/src/db/queries.ts` with list/get/update functions for `agent_definitions`, `instruction_files`, and `skill_definitions`.

steps.task: Expose DB entity CRUD via `dashboard/server/src/routes/agents.ts`, `instructions.ts`, and create a new `skills.ts` route. Register the new router in `index.ts`.
steps.notes: Exposed DB entity CRUD via `dashboard/server/src/routes/agents.ts`, `instructions.ts`, and created a new `skills.ts` route. Registered the new router in `index.ts`. (Completed in Phase 1 execution).

steps.phase: Phase 2: Frontend Implementation - Database Context View
steps.task: Update `dashboard/src/pages/AgentsPage.tsx` and `InstructionsPage.tsx` to support a \"Database\" mode/tab that fetches from `/api/*/db`.
steps.notes: Updated `dashboard/src/pages/AgentsPage.tsx` and `InstructionsPage.tsx` to support a "Database" mode that fetches from `/api/*/db`. Created `dashboard/src/pages/SkillsPage.tsx` for database-stored skills. Updated sidebar navigation. Created `DbEntityEditorPage.tsx` for generic DB entity editing. Resolved all TypeScript and build issues.

steps.phase: Phase 2: Frontend Implementation - Database Context View
steps.task: Create `dashboard/src/pages/SkillsPage.tsx` to manage skill definitions stored in the DB. Add it to the main navigation.
steps.notes: Created `dashboard/src/pages/SkillsPage.tsx` and added it to the sidebar. (Completed during previous step).

steps.phase: Phase 2: Frontend Implementation - Database Context View
steps.task: Implement/Update `dashboard/src/pages/AgentEditorPage.tsx` (or create a generic entity editor) to support saving content back to the DB entity update endpoints.
steps.notes: Created `dashboard/src/pages/DbEntityEditorPage.tsx` which handles editing for all DB entities (Agents, Instructions, Skills). Integrated it into the routing.

steps.task: Build the dashboard and verify that DB-stored entities can be viewed, edited, and saved correctly. Check for case-sensitivity and metadata handling.
steps.notes: Successfully built and verified the dashboard. All DB entity management features are operational and TypeScript errors are resolved. Plan complete.
